text: Human Resources (HR) are the main source of success in an organization. They connect technological and information resources to output. This study provides an overview of the theory of Human Resource Management (HRM). It defines the process of planning, organizing, executing and controlling the human resources in the organization in the most efficient and effective way, while explaining the double structural function of the HRM that comprises the general management functions such as planning, organizing, directing and controlling in addition to the specific operational functions such as employment, training, wages, induction, personnel maintenance and discharge. The study focuses highly on the job analysis function during the planning stage of the personnel activities. This is because the job analysis in an organization is considered a very important function for the purpose of determining the job duties and the behaviour criteria required from the employees to ensure the efficiency of the organization performance. Hence, the importance of good management of human resources to avoid any bureaucratic Bottlenecks and to ensure the adequate placement of the skilled manpower to achieve the objectives of the organization.
